query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mellum2 technical report" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on fine-tuning strategies for LLM agents using negative examples (failed trajectories), which is a methodological contribution to agent training rather than a study of the specific "Mellum2" linguistics framework or the "llmXive" follow-up context requested. It does not measure the same biological/algorithmic mechanism, share the same empirical population, or serve as a foundational reference for the specific linguistic extension the user is investigating.
